content.text:
*brief pause*

I guess because it doesn't need to be me doing it. Like, the operational stuff — I have people for that. I know that. And yet I'm the one who ends up in the weeds on it.

*slight tilt of the head*

It's not even that I enjoy it more. It's just... easier to start, maybe. The launch has more moving pieces, so maybe it's just a complexity thing. Like my brain defaults to the simpler task.

That's probably it, actually. It's not emotional, it's just — task switching is hard when something has a lot of dependencies. I've read about that. ADHD stuff, kind of, even though I've never been diagnosed.
